name: Zucchini Stuffed with Bulgur and Ground Turkey
recipeInstructions:
1. Place zucchini in a large deep skillet and add enough boiling water to cover.
2. Cook for 10 to 12 minutes, then chill quickly under cold water. Cut off the stem end of each zucchini and split lengthwise.
3. Starting from the wide end of the vegetable, scoop out pulp with a spoon, leaving a 1/2-inch thick shell; reserve the pulp. Place shells in a 9x13 baking dish.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
5. In a food processor, mince scallions, green pepper, and parsley. Add drained tomatoes (reserve liquid), reserved zucchini pulp, ground turkey, spices, and bulgur; process until well mixed.
6. Generously spoon the mixture into scooped zucchini shells. Add enough reserved tomato liquid to make 1 cup, stir in a little tomato paste, and pour around the bottom of the baking dish. Cover pan with foil.
7. Bake for 35 minutes, removing foil every 10 minutes to baste with liquid. Remove foil completely for the last 5 minutes of baking.
8. Serve hot with lemon wedges.
